subject: AP Physics 1
custom_notes: Reviewed Unit 7 Oscillations bank with answer-only formatting for import and minor question cleanup.

questionTypes.type: Identify the relationship between pendulum length and period

questions.question: A simple pendulum has a period T on Earth. If the length of the pendulum is increased by a factor of 4, what is the new period?
Two pendulums, A and B, are created. Pendulum A is twice as long as Pendulum B. How does the period of A compare to B?
To triple the period of a simple pendulum, the length must be changed by what factor?
If the length of a pendulum is reduced to one-fourth of its original length, the frequency of oscillation will:

questionTypes.type: Determine the effect of mass and amplitude on pendulum period

questions.question: A pendulum bob is replaced with a new sphere of the same size but greater density. If it is set into oscillation with the same angle, how does the new period compare?
A student performs an experiment to determine if the period of a pendulum depends on the mass. What should be the independent and dependent variables?
Which of the following changes will result in a change in the period of a simple pendulum?
options: Increasing the mass of the bob
Increasing the amplitude from 5 to 10 degrees
Moving the pendulum to a planet with higher gravity
Decreasing the mass of the bob
questions.correct_option: Moving the pendulum to a planet with higher gravity
questions.answer: C. Moving the pendulum to a planet with higher gravity

questions.question: A 1 kg mass on a 1 m string has period T. A 2 kg mass on a 1 m string is pulled to the same 5-degree angle. Its period is:

questionTypes.type: Calculate the period of a mass-spring system from m and k

questions.question: A 0.125 kg object is attached to a spring. If the system oscillates such that it completes 2 full cycles in 2 seconds, what is the period?
If the mass attached to a spring is doubled and the spring constant is also doubled, the period of oscillation:
A mass m on a spring with constant k has period T. What is the period if the mass is increased to 4m?
To halve the period of a mass-spring system without changing the mass, the spring constant k must be:

questionTypes.type: Analyze position vs. time graphs for SHM

questions.question: A position-time graph for an oscillator shows a peak at 0.5 s and the next peak at 1.5 s. What is the frequency of oscillation?
On a position vs. time graph for simple harmonic motion, at what point is the object's speed greatest?
If the amplitude of a simple harmonic oscillator is doubled, which feature of the position-time graph changes?
options: The vertical distance from equilibrium to peak
questions.correct_option: The vertical distance from equilibrium to peak
questions.answer: C. The vertical distance from equilibrium to peak

questions.question: A graph shows position x = A cos(bt). The constant 'b' is related to which physical property?

questionTypes.type: Relate velocity vs. time and acceleration vs. time graphs

questions.question: When the velocity of an oscillating object is at its maximum positive value, what is the object's acceleration?
An acceleration vs. time graph for SHM is a reflection of which other graph across the time axis (with scaling)?
At the turning points of an oscillation (maximum displacement), which statement is true?
options: Velocity is max, acceleration is zero
Velocity is zero, acceleration is max
questions.correct_option: Velocity is zero, acceleration is max
questions.answer: B. Velocity is zero, acceleration is max

questions.question: A velocity vs. time graph shows a sine wave starting at the origin. What is the phase of the position graph?
options: Negative cosine wave starting at trough
questions.correct_option: Negative cosine wave starting at trough
questions.answer: C. Negative cosine wave starting at trough

questionTypes.type: Determine potential and kinetic energy in SHM

questions.question: In a frictionless mass-spring system, at what position is the kinetic energy equal to the total mechanical energy?
If the total energy of an oscillating system is E, what is the spring potential energy when the mass is at its maximum displacement?
A mass-spring system is compressed by a distance X. If it is compressed by 2X instead, how does the total mechanical energy change?
As an object moves from the equilibrium position toward maximum extension, what energy transformation occurs?

questionTypes.type: Identify properties of the restoring force for ideal springs

questions.question: Which of the following force-displacement relationships will result in simple harmonic motion?
A student tests a rubber band and finds that the force required to stretch it is proportional to x^2. Is the resulting motion simple harmonic?
options: Yes, because it is a restoring force
No, because the force is not linear with displacement
No, because rubber bands have no mass
questions.correct_option: No, because the force is not linear with displacement
questions.answer: B. No, because the force is not linear with displacement

questions.question: The slope of a graph of Force vs. Displacement for an ideal spring represents:
In the equation F = -kx, the negative sign indicates:
options: The force points opposite to the displacement
questions.correct_option: The force points opposite to the displacement
questions.answer: C. The force points opposite to the displacement

questionTypes.type: Design experiments for oscillatory systems

questions.question: To determine the spring constant k of a vertical spring, a student should measure:
options: Initial length and final length with a known mass
The color and thickness of the spring
The maximum speed of the mass
questions.correct_option: Initial length and final length with a known mass
questions.answer: A. Initial length and final length with a known mass

questions.question: Which tool is best for determining the period of a fast-oscillating spring with high precision?
options: Human observation with a wall clock

questions.question: A student wants to find 'g' using a pendulum. Which graph will have a slope that allows 'g' to be calculated easily?
In an experiment to find 'k', a student plots Force (F) on the y-axis and displacement (x) on the x-axis. The intercept is zero. This indicates:
options: The spring is ideal and follows Hooke's Law
questions.correct_option: The spring is ideal and follows Hooke's Law
questions.answer: B. The spring is ideal and follows Hooke's Law

questionTypes.type: Predict changes in period or energy using functional dependence

questions.question: A mass-spring system has period T on Earth. On the Moon, where gravity is 1/6 that of Earth, its period will be:
If the amplitude of oscillation for a mass-spring system is doubled, how does the period change?
An object's mass is tripled in a mass-spring system. The new frequency is what factor of the old frequency?
Two springs are identical except spring A is twice as stiff as spring B (k_A = 2k_B). If same mass m is used, how do the periods compare?

questionTypes.type: Calculate work and energy from force-displacement graphs

questions.question: A force vs. displacement graph for a spring shows a line from (0,0) to (0.1 m, 10 N). How much work was done to stretch the spring?
A block is released from rest after compressing a spring. The energy with which the block leaves the spring is determined by:
options: The area under the force-displacement graph during compression
questions.correct_option: The area under the force-displacement graph during compression
questions.answer: C. The area under the force-displacement graph during compression

questions.question: A spring with constant 200 N/m is compressed 0.2 m. What is the stored potential energy?
If a force-displacement graph is non-linear (e.g., F = bx^2), how is the potential energy found?
options: Calculating the area under the curve
Multiplying final force by final displacement
questions.correct_option: Calculating the area under the curve
questions.answer: B. Calculating the area under the curve

questionTypes.type: Identify phase relationships and turning points in SHM

questions.question: At the moment an oscillating object passes through the equilibrium position, which quantity is at its MINIMUM magnitude?
If an object is at its maximum positive displacement, what is the direction of its acceleration?
A system is released from rest at x = +A. Describe its motion immediately after release.
In one full cycle of SHM, how many times does the object reach its maximum speed?

questionTypes.type: Compare vertical and horizontal mass-spring systems

questions.question: How does the period of a mass-spring system on a horizontal frictionless table compare to the same mass and spring hanging vertically?
In a vertical mass-spring system, the equilibrium position is located where:
options: The spring force equals the weight of the mass
The potential energy is zero by definition
The mass is at the bottom of its motion
questions.correct_option: The spring force equals the weight of the mass
questions.answer: B. The spring force equals the weight of the mass

questions.question: If a vertical spring system is moved to a planet with higher gravity, how does the equilibrium position change?
For a vertical spring, the 'restoring force' that causes SHM is the:
options: Net force of spring and gravity
questions.correct_option: Net force of spring and gravity
questions.answer: C. Net force of spring and gravity

questionTypes.type: Analyze energy transformations in SHM

questions.question: Which graph correctly represents Kinetic Energy (K) as a function of Position (x) for SHM?
options: Parabola opening down with peak at x=0
Parabola opening up with vertex at x=0
questions.correct_option: Parabola opening down with peak at x=0
questions.answer: C. Parabola opening down with peak at x=0

questions.question: When an oscillating mass is at x = A/2 (half the amplitude), what fraction of the total energy is potential energy?
At what position is the energy of a mass-spring system equally split between kinetic and potential?
If friction is present, the total mechanical energy of the oscillator will:

questionTypes.type: Solve for instantaneous position or velocity using conservation of energy

questions.question: A mass m on a spring with constant k is released from rest at x=A. What is its speed at x=0?
A pendulum bob is released from height H. What is its speed at the lowest point?
If a mass-spring system has total energy E, and at a certain point its potential energy is 0.25E, what is its kinetic energy?
A spring is compressed by X and shoots a mass m. If the mass is doubled, the launch speed will change by what factor?

questionTypes.type: Relate pendulum motion to non-inertial frames

questions.question: A physicist in an airplane that is accelerating horizontally at rate 'a' observes a pendulum bob. The string makes an angle theta with the vertical. This angle is determined by:
In an elevator accelerating upward, the period of a pendulum will:
A pendulum is in a car traveling at constant velocity. The string is:
If a pendulum is in free fall (a = g downward), its period is:

questionTypes.type: Analyze the motion of a system with multiple mass/spring configurations

questions.question: Two identical springs are connected in parallel to a single mass m. The effective spring constant of the system is:
Two identical springs are connected in series to a single mass m. The effective spring constant of the system is:
A mass m is attached to a spring and oscillates with frequency f. If a second identical mass is glued to the first, the new frequency is:
A block of mass M is attached to a spring of constant K. A smaller block is placed on top of it. The period will:

questionTypes.type: Determine tension in a swinging pendulum bob

questions.question: At which position in a pendulum's swing is the tension in the string greatest?
A pendulum of mass m is released from height H. At the lowest point, the tension is:
If a pendulum is released from an angle such that the bob's height is L, at the bottom the tension is 3mg. Is this possible?
options: No, tension is independent of height

questions.question: At the highest point of its swing (turning point), the tension is:

questionTypes.type: Calculate frequency and angular frequency

questions.question: A system completes 120 oscillations in one minute. What is its frequency in Hz?
If the period of an oscillator is 0.1 s, what is its angular frequency (omega)?
Which of the following is equivalent to the frequency 'f' of a mass-spring system?
If the frequency of a system is 5 Hz, how long does one cycle take?

questionTypes.type: Determine maximum speed and acceleration

questions.question: A simple harmonic oscillator has amplitude A and angular frequency omega. Its maximum speed is:
The maximum acceleration of an oscillating object is proportional to:
A mass-spring system with amplitude 0.1 m and frequency 2 Hz has a max speed of approximately:
To double the maximum acceleration of an oscillator without changing the frequency, the amplitude must be:

questionTypes.type: Evaluate energy in non-ideal oscillators

questions.question: A student finds that the amplitude of a pendulum decreases over time. What can be concluded about the total energy of the system?
In a real (non-ideal) oscillating system, where does the 'lost' mechanical energy go?
options: Converted to thermal energy in the surroundings
questions.correct_option: Converted to thermal energy in the surroundings
questions.answer: C. Converted to thermal energy in the surroundings

questions.question: A pendulum swings through air with noticeable damping. Compared with ideal motion, which change is most directly caused by air resistance?
questions.answer: A. Its amplitude decreases each cycle

questions.question: A rubber band oscillator is stretched. If heat is generated during the stretch, the efficiency of energy conversion is:
